stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In a 2024 study, agricultural ecologist Ravi Mehta and colleagues tested the use of intercropped lavender rows in California vineyards.
  • Intercropping involves planting a secondary crop between rows of a primary crop.
  • Finding: vineyards with lavender rows showed 48% greater beneficial-insect activity than control vineyards.
  • Finding: each square meter of lavender row produced approximately 70 grams of marketable dried lavender.
stem: The student wants to present the findings of the study. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The authors concluded that lavender intercropping was "commercially viable," having produced 70 grams per square meter of marketable dried lavender in a 2024 study.
In a 2024 study, Mehta and colleagues found that intercropped lavender produced 48% more dried lavender than the control.
Mehta and colleagues found that intercropping lavender in vineyards not only increased beneficial-insect activity but also produced marketable dried lavender.
Intercropped lavender rows, Mehta and colleagues found, yielded 70 grams of dried lavender and reduced 48% of pests in vineyards.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because it presents both findings—greater beneficial-insect activity and marketable dried lavender—accurately.

Choice A draws a conclusion the notes don't support. Choice B applies the 48% figure to the wrong variable. Choice D mischaracterizes the 48% figure as pest reduction.
